name: Potage St. Germain
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the drained, dried peas in a soup kettle.
2. Add the chicken broth; bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
4. Skim any froth from the top of the soup.
5. Melt butter in a skillet.
6. Saute carrots and onions until the onions are golden brown.
7. Add leeks and lettuce; simmer on low heat for 10 minutes.
8. Stir the vegetable mixture into the soup.
9. Add sugar and salt to the soup.
10. Return the soup to a simmer and let it cook until the split peas are tender, about 2 hours.
11. Puree the fresh peas using an immersion blender or sieve.
12. Purée the soup mixture; return it to the kettle.
13. Stir in cream and milk.
14. Heat gently, stirring occasionally to prevent scorching.
15. Just before serving, add butter.
16. Garnish with a dollop of sour cream, a dash of sherry, or both.
